can you please execute this linear ticket?

Work on Linear issue ENG-7505:

<issue identifier="ENG-7505">
<title>Add `leo50` to `view_prospect_to_org_journey`</title>
<description>
**Context**

See slack alert: [https://usestable.slack.com/archives/C092UG0SUBD/p1772438410835439](<https://usestable.slack.com/archives/C092UG0SUBD/p1772438410835439>)

![CleanShot 2026-03-04 at 11.35.07@2x.png](https://uploads.linear.app/bf89eb10-62b7-4fe4-a054-24721cd6de52/f82fcd06-a901-43dc-a380-9d291944eca3/6c11e7d6-a378-4492-b92c-b8d60fcb5fe5?signature=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJwYXRoIjoiL2JmODllYjEwLTYyYjctNGZlNC1hMDU0LTI0NzIxY2Q2ZGU1Mi9mODJmY2QwNi1hOTAxLTQzZGMtYTM4MC05ZDI5MTk0NGVjYTMvNmMxMWU3ZDYtYTM3OC00NDkyLWI5MmMtYjhkNjBmY2I1ZmU1IiwiaWF0IjoxNzcyNjQyMzQ1LCJleHAiOjE3NzI2ODU1NDV9.yxFKq8Rdov81kE6KC9mN3JfUSStMgOBOhl79VFj-2AA)

Add `leo50` to the list of Marketing (Discount Code)'s in `view_prospect_to_org_journey` table.
</description>
<team name="Engineering"/>
</issue>
I'll help you add `leo50` to the `view_prospect_to_org_journey` table. Let me start by understanding the current structure and locating where Marketing discount codes are defined.

content.content:
1→/*
     2→================================================================================
     3→TABLE: view_prospect_to_org_journey
     4→PURPOSE: Combined view of prospects and organizations with promo code categorization and MRR data
     5→================================================================================
     6→
     7→DESCRIPTION:
     8→This table provides a unified view of the customer journey from prospect to organization,
     9→including all records from both mysql_prospects and mysql_organizations tables.
    10→It applies promo code categorization logic directly to prospects and includes
    11→all columns from both tables (prospect columns prefixed with prospect_, org columns prefixed with org_).
    12→Additionally includes the initial positive MRR date from MRR data for conversion tracking.
    13→
    14→GRANULARITY:
    15→One row per combined prospect/organization record, including:
    16→- Matched prospect-organization relationships (prospect and org columns populated). When a prospect has multiple organizations, the prospect data will be duplicated across multiple rows (one per organization).
    17→- Prospects without any related organization (all org_* columns NULL).
    18→- Organizations without any related prospect (all prospect_* columns NULL).
    19→
    20→IMPORTANT - DUPLICATE WARNING:
    21→This view can contain duplicate prospect_id values when a single prospect is associated 
    22→with multiple organizations. Always use COUNT(DISTINCT prospect_id) when counting unique 
    23→prospects to avoid inflated counts. The org_stripeid values are unique (no duplicates).
    24→
    25→As of 2026-02-11, the distribution of prospect-to-organization relationships:
    26→- 27,245 prospects have 1 organization (normal case)
    27→- 55 prospects have 2 organizations
    28→- 5 prospects have 3 organizations
    29→- 1 prospect has 7 organizations (extreme edge case)
    30→
    31→KEY COLUMNS:
    32→- prospect_* columns: All columns from mysql_prospects table prefixed with prospect_ (Airbyte columns excluded)
    33→- org_* columns: All columns from mysql_organizations table prefixed with org_ (Airbyte columns excluded)
    34→- initial_positive_mrr_date: Date when customer first had positive MRR (NULL if never had positive MRR)
    35→- promo_code_type: Categorized promo code type (YC, Stripe, Referral (Other Partners), Customer Referral, Marketing (No Promo Code), Marketing (Discount Code), No Related Prospect, or NULL)
    36→  - NULL/empty prospects get 'Marketing (No Promo Code)' category
    37→  - Specific marketing codes get 'Marketing (Discount Code)' category
    38→  - Codes ending in 'stref' get 'Customer Referral' category
    39→  - Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect' category
    40→  - Uncategorized promo codes get NULL
    41→- utm_type: Categorized UTM medium type (Marketing (Organic), Marketing (Paid), or No Related Prospect)
    42→  - NULL/empty medium gets 'Marketing (Organic)' category
    43→  - Paid channels (cpc, ppc, paidsocial, paid, newsletter, display, cpc\\, podcast) get 'Marketing (Paid)' category
    44→  - Organic channels (organic, social, email, referral, post, description, card, getro.com) get 'Marketing (Organic)' category
    45→  - Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect' category
    46→- marketing_attribution: Final marketing attribution combining promo code and UTM data
    47→  - YC, Stripe, Referral (Other Partners) keep their promo_code_type values
    48→  - Marketing categories and Customer Referral use their utm_type values
    49→  - Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect' category
    50→
    51→DEPENDENCIES:
    52→- public.mysql_prospects: Source table with prospect data
    53→- public.mysql_organizations: Source table with organization data
    54→- public.view_mrr_by_customer_by_day: MRR history for initial positive MRR date
    55→
    56→LAST MODIFIED: 2026-02-19
    57→OWNER: Data Team
    58→================================================================================
    59→*/
    60→
    61→-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
    62→DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_prospect_to_org_journey_new;
    63→
    64→-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
    65→CREATE TABLE view_prospect_to_org_journey_new
    66→DISTKEY(prospect_id)
    67→SORTKEY(prospect_createdat, org_createdat)
    68→AS
    69→WITH initial_positive_mrr_dates AS (
    70→    /*
    71→    Get the first day each customer had positive MRR from the view_mrr_by_customer_by_day table.
    72→    
    73→    WHY THIS APPROACH:
    74→    - We need to identify when customers first had positive MRR (not just when they signed up)
    75→    - Many customers start with trials or free tiers before having positive MRR
    76→    - Using MIN(event_day) WHERE mrr > 0 gives us the first date with positive MRR
    77→    - This is more accurate than using day_number = 0 (which could be a trial start with $0 MRR)
    78→    - This is more accurate than active_start_mrr_change (which can occur multiple times per customer)
    79→    
    80→    DATA SOURCE:
    81→    - view_mrr_by_customer_by_day: Our source of truth for MRR history
    82→    - Contains one row per customer per day from their first Stripe event to latest
    83→    - MRR values are forward-filled (last observation carried forward)
    84→    
    85→    ACCURACY NOTE:
    86→    When compared to counting customers directly from view_mrr_by_customer_by_day,
    87→    this join approach produces nearly identical results (99.3%+ match rate).
    88→    Small discrepancies (<0.7% in any given month) may occur due to:
    89→    - Customer IDs in MRR data without corresponding organizations
    90→    - Data pipeline timing differences between tables
    91→    - Edge cases where organizations exist without Stripe customers
    92→    
    93→    Example validation (2024-2026 data):
    94→    - Direct from MRR view: 24,866 total customers with positive MRR
    95→    - Via this join: 24,852 total customers with positive MRR  
    96→    - Difference: 14 customers (0.056%)
    97→    */
    98→    SELECT 
    99→        customer_id,
   100→        MIN(event_day) AS initial_positive_mrr_date
   101→    FROM public.view_mrr_by_customer_by_day
   102→    WHERE mrr > 0
   103→    GROUP BY customer_id
   104→),
   105→base_data AS (
   106→    SELECT 
   107→    -- Prospect columns (prefixed with prospect_, excluding Airbyte columns)
   108→    p.id AS prospect_id,
   109→    p.plan AS prospect_plan,
   110→    p.email AS prospect_email,
   111→    p.gclid AS prospect_gclid,
   112→    p.notes AS prospect_notes,
   113→    p.state AS prospect_state,
   114→    p.fbclid AS prospect_fbclid,
   115→    p.medium AS prospect_medium,
   116→    p.source AS prospect_source,
   117→    p.keyword AS prospect_keyword,
   118→    p.campaign AS prospect_campaign,
   119→    p.interval AS prospect_interval,
   120→    p.lastname AS prospect_lastname,
   121→    p.referral AS prospect_referral,
   122→    p.adgroupid AS prospect_adgroupid,
   123→    p.createdat AS prospect_createdat,
   124→    p.firstname AS prospect_firstname,
   125→    p.partnerid AS prospect_partnerid,
   126→    p.promocode AS prospect_promocode,
   127→    p.updatedat AS prospect_updatedat,
   128→    p.campaignid AS prospect_campaignid,
   129→    p.gaclientid AS prospect_gaclientid,
   130→    p.companyname AS prospect_companyname,
   131→    p.initialstate AS prospect_initialstate,
   132→    p.locationcode AS prospect_locationcode,
   133→    p.referrerhost AS prospect_referrerhost,
   134→    p.referrerpath AS prospect_referrerpath,
   135→    p.stablesource AS prospect_stablesource,
   136→    p.promocodename AS prospect_promocodename,
   137→    p.qualification AS prospect_qualification,
   138→    p.referrersearch AS prospect_referrersearch,
   139→    p.landingpagehost AS prospect_landingpagehost,
   140→    p.landingpagepath AS prospect_landingpagepath,
   141→    p.registeredagents AS prospect_registeredagents,
   142→    p.stripecustomerid AS prospect_stripecustomerid,
   143→    p.clearbitpersonkey AS prospect_clearbitpersonkey,
   144→    p.landingpagesearch AS prospect_landingpagesearch,
   145→    p.clearbitcompanykey AS prospect_clearbitcompanykey,
   146→    p.initiallocationcode AS prospect_initiallocationcode,
   147→    p.failedpaymentmethodhold AS prospect_failedpaymentmethodhold,
   148→    
   149→    -- Organization columns (prefixed with org_, excluding Airbyte columns)
   150→    o.id AS org_id,
   151→    o.name AS org_name,
   152→    o.hasbaa AS org_hasbaa,
   153→    o.nickname AS org_nickname,
   154→    o.stripeid AS org_stripeid,
   155→    o.createdat AS org_createdat,
   156→    o.updatedat AS org_updatedat,
   157→    o.prospectid AS org_prospectid,
   158→    o.outboundwebhookid AS org_outboundwebhookid,
   159→    
   160→    -- Promo code categorization logic
   161→    CASE 
   162→        -- Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect'
   163→        WHEN p.id IS NULL AND o.id IS NOT NULL THEN 'No Related Prospect'
   164→        -- Apply categorization for prospects
   165→        WHEN p.id IS NOT NULL THEN
   166→            CASE 
   167→                -- YC codes get 'YC' category
   168→                WHEN p.promocodename = 'yc'
   169→                    OR p.promocodename = 'ycombinator'
   170→                    OR p.promocodename ~ '^yc[fswx][0-9]{2}$'
   171→                THEN 'YC'
   172→                
   173→                -- Stripe Atlas codes get 'Stripe' category
   174→                WHEN p.promocodename ~ '^stripe(atlas|partner|atlaspartner)$'
   175→                THEN 'Stripe'
   176→                
   177→                -- Other referral partner codes get 'Referral (Other Partners)' category
   178→                WHEN p.promocodename IN (
   179→                    'mercury',
   180→                    'levy',
   181→                    'chore',
   182→                    'doola',
   183→                    'central',
   184→                    'pocketbook',
   185→                    'blinkdigital',
   186→                    'producthunt50',
   187→                    'codi50',
   188→                    'remofirst50',
   189→                    'clerkydeal',
   190→                    'openphone',
   191→                    'arightco',
   192→                    'rocketlawyerf50',
   193→                    'mosey',
   194→                    'speedinvest',
   195→                    'vera',
   196→                    'f6s',
   197→                    'casium',
   198→                    'gatewayx',
   199→                    'northone',
   200→                    'northoneplus',
   201→                    'everyio',
   202→                    'goprint',
   203→                    'seamess',
   204→                    'flex',
   205→                    'justworks',
   206→                    'cvconcepts',
   207→                    'moxe',
   208→                    'kmg',
   209→                    'fptrial50',
   210→                    'founderpass50grow',
   211→                    'briars',
   212→                    'thetaxvalet',
   213→                    'pilot',
   214→                    'cfoadvisors',
   215→                    'ledgerly',
   216→                    'cfoproject',
   217→                    'burkland',
   218→                    'buildingprofits',
   219→                    'inventus',
   220→                    'inventuslaw',
   221→                    'wsgr',
   222→                    'gunderson',
   223→                    'cytlaw',
   224→                    'epigram',
   225→                    'ashbury',
   226→                    'flowfi',
   227→                    'fusion',
   228→                    'deel',
   229→                    'brex',
   230→                    'pulley',
   231→                    'clerky',
   232→                    'hustlefund',
   233→                    'sus',
   234→                    '500startups',
   235→                    'skydeck',
   236→                    'ondeck',
   237→                    'acceleprise',
   238→                    'villageglobal',
   239→                    'aircfo',
   240→                    'magicbell',
   241→                    'southparkcommons',
   242→                    'levro',
   243→                    'talentdrop',
   244→                    'producthunt',
   245→                    'tacostars',
   246→                    'lombardstreet',
   247→                    'fondo',
   248→                    'stablefam',
   249→                    'precursor20',
   250→                    'precursor50'
   251→                ) THEN 'Referral (Other Partners)'
   252→                
   253→                -- Customer Referral codes (ending in 'stref')
   254→                WHEN p.promocodename ~ 'stref$' THEN 'Customer Referral'
   255→                
   256→                -- Marketing (No Promo Code) - NULL or empty promo codes
   257→                WHEN COALESCE(p.promocodename, '') = '' THEN 'Marketing (No Promo Code)'
   258→                
   259→                -- Marketing (Discount Code) - specific marketing campaign codes
   260→                WHEN p.promocodename IN (
   261→                        '100creator2025',
   262→                        '3monthsfree',
   263→                        '50pros',
   264→                        'abstractops',
   265→                        'allthehacks',
   266→                        'business50',
   267→                        'cooley',
   268→                        'customplan',
   269→                        'efficient',
   270→                        'emsoft',
   271→                        'eoy25',
   272→                        'fall50',
   273→                        'florida',
   274→                        'formation',
   275→                        'gdsvfh',
   276→                        'https://dashboard.usestable.com/onboard/begin?',
   277→                        'katie50',
   278→                        'kelly50',
   279→                        'killb',
   280→                        'novo',
   281→                        'ph50',
   282→                        'pod',
   283→                        'redesignhealth',
   284→                        'remote',
   285→                        'scale20',
   286→                        'scale50',
   287→                        'stableil2024',
   288→                        'stablema2024',
   289→                        'stablewy2024',
   290→                        'swap20',
   291→                        'texas',
   292→                        'tldr50',
   293→                        'worklife'
   294→                    ) THEN 'Marketing (Discount Code)'
   295→                
   296→                -- Everything else gets NULL (uncategorized/new codes)
   297→                ELSE NULL
   298→            END
   299→        -- This shouldn't happen in a FULL OUTER JOIN, but keeping for completeness
   300→        ELSE NULL
   301→    END AS promo_code_type,
   302→    
   303→    -- UTM Type categorization based on medium field
   304→    CASE 
   305→        -- Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect'
   306→        WHEN p.id IS NULL AND o.id IS NOT NULL THEN 'No Related Prospect'
   307→        -- Apply categorization for prospects
   308→        WHEN p.id IS NOT NULL THEN
   309→            CASE 
   310→                -- Paid channels get 'Marketing (Paid)' category
   311→                WHEN p.medium IN (
   312→                    'cpc',
   313→                    'ppc', 
   314→                    'paidsocial',
   315→                    'paid',
   316→                    'newsletter',
   317→                    'display',
   318→                    'cpc\\',
   319→                    'podcast'
   320→                ) THEN 'Marketing (Paid)'
   321→                
   322→                -- Everything else (including NULL/empty and organic channels) gets 'Marketing (Organic)'
   323→                -- Explicitly listed organic channels: organic, social, email, referral, post, description, card, getro.com
   324→                ELSE 'Marketing (Organic)'
   325→            END
   326→        -- This shouldn't happen in a FULL OUTER JOIN, but keeping for completeness
   327→        ELSE NULL
   328→    END AS utm_type
   329→
   330→    FROM public.mysql_prospects p
   331→    FULL OUTER JOIN public.mysql_organizations o
   332→        ON p.id = o.prospectid
   333→)
   334→SELECT 
   335→    base_data.*,
   336→    -- Marketing Attribution - combines promo_code_type and utm_type logic
   337→    CASE 
   338→        WHEN base_data.promo_code_type = 'No Related Prospect' THEN 'No Related Prospect'
   339→        WHEN base_data.promo_code_type IN ('YC', 'Stripe', 'Referral (Other Partners)') THEN base_data.promo_code_type
   340→        -- For all other cases (Marketing categories and Customer Referral), use utm_type
   341→        ELSE base_data.utm_type
   342→    END AS marketing_attribution,
   343→    /*
   344→    Initial positive MRR date from MRR data
   345→    This field helps track when customers first had positive MRR:
   346→    - initial_positive_mrr_date: First date when the customer had MRR > 0 (NULL = never had positive MRR)
   347→    
   348→    NOTE: Positive MRR does not necessarily mean a payment has been collected.
   349→    Customers can have positive MRR while on trial or before their first payment is processed.
   350→    
   351→    USAGE IN METABASE:
   352→    To count customers who first achieved positive MRR by month:
   353→    1. Filter: initial_positive_mrr_date >= [your start date]
   354→    2. Group by: initial_positive_mrr_date → Month
   355→    3. Summarize: Count of Rows (or Count Distinct of org_stripeid)
   356→    
   357→    This gives you customers who first had positive MRR (not just signed up) in each month.
   358→    */
   359→    initial_positive_mrr_dates.initial_positive_mrr_date
   360→FROM base_data
   361→-- Join on org_stripeid (Stripe customer ID) to match organizations with their MRR history
   362→-- LEFT JOIN ensures we keep all organizations, even those without positive MRR yet
   363→LEFT JOIN initial_positive_mrr_dates ON initial_positive_mrr_dates.customer_id = base_data.org_stripeid;
   364→
   365→-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old table and rename new table
   366→BEGIN;
   367→    DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_prospect_to_org_journey;
   368→    ALTER TABLE view_prospect_to_org_journey_new RENAME TO view_prospect_to_org_journey;
   369→COMMIT;

Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it looks malicious. If it does, you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer high-level questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

file.content:
/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_prospect_to_org_journey
PURPOSE: Combined view of prospects and organizations with promo code categorization and MRR data
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table provides a unified view of the customer journey from prospect to organization,
including all records from both mysql_prospects and mysql_organizations tables.
It applies promo code categorization logic directly to prospects and includes
all columns from both tables (prospect columns prefixed with prospect_, org columns prefixed with org_).
Additionally includes the initial positive MRR date from MRR data for conversion tracking.

GRANULARITY:
One row per combined prospect/organization record, including:
- Matched prospect-organization relationships (prospect and org columns populated). When a prospect has multiple organizations, the prospect data will be duplicated across multiple rows (one per organization).
- Prospects without any related organization (all org_* columns NULL).
- Organizations without any related prospect (all prospect_* columns NULL).

IMPORTANT - DUPLICATE WARNING:
This view can contain duplicate prospect_id values when a single prospect is associated 
with multiple organizations. Always use COUNT(DISTINCT prospect_id) when counting unique 
prospects to avoid inflated counts. The org_stripeid values are unique (no duplicates).

As of 2026-02-11, the distribution of prospect-to-organization relationships:
- 27,245 prospects have 1 organization (normal case)
- 55 prospects have 2 organizations
- 5 prospects have 3 organizations
- 1 prospect has 7 organizations (extreme edge case)

KEY COLUMNS:
- prospect_* columns: All columns from mysql_prospects table prefixed with prospect_ (Airbyte columns excluded)
- org_* columns: All columns from mysql_organizations table prefixed with org_ (Airbyte columns excluded)
- initial_positive_mrr_date: Date when customer first had positive MRR (NULL if never had positive MRR)
- promo_code_type: Categorized promo code type (YC, Stripe, Referral (Other Partners), Customer Referral, Marketing (No Promo Code), Marketing (Discount Code), No Related Prospect, or NULL)
  - NULL/empty prospects get 'Marketing (No Promo Code)' category
  - Specific marketing codes get 'Marketing (Discount Code)' category
  - Codes ending in 'stref' get 'Customer Referral' category
  - Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect' category
  - Uncategorized promo codes get NULL
- utm_type: Categorized UTM medium type (Marketing (Organic), Marketing (Paid), or No Related Prospect)
  - NULL/empty medium gets 'Marketing (Organic)' category
  - Paid channels (cpc, ppc, paidsocial, paid, newsletter, display, cpc\\, podcast) get 'Marketing (Paid)' category
  - Organic channels (organic, social, email, referral, post, description, card, getro.com) get 'Marketing (Organic)' category
  - Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect' category
- marketing_attribution: Final marketing attribution combining promo code and UTM data
  - YC, Stripe, Referral (Other Partners) keep their promo_code_type values
  - Marketing categories and Customer Referral use their utm_type values
  - Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect' category

DEPENDENCIES:
- public.mysql_prospects: Source table with prospect data
- public.mysql_organizations: Source table with organization data
- public.view_mrr_by_customer_by_day: MRR history for initial positive MRR date

LAST MODIFIED: 2026-02-19
OWNER: Data Team
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_prospect_to_org_journey_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_prospect_to_org_journey_new
DISTKEY(prospect_id)
SORTKEY(prospect_createdat, org_createdat)
AS
WITH initial_positive_mrr_dates AS (
    /*
    Get the first day each customer had positive MRR from the view_mrr_by_customer_by_day table.
    
    WHY THIS APPROACH:
    - We need to identify when customers first had positive MRR (not just when they signed up)
    - Many customers start with trials or free tiers before having positive MRR
    - Using MIN(event_day) WHERE mrr > 0 gives us the first date with positive MRR
    - This is more accurate than using day_number = 0 (which could be a trial start with $0 MRR)
    - This is more accurate than active_start_mrr_change (which can occur multiple times per customer)
    
    DATA SOURCE:
    - view_mrr_by_customer_by_day: Our source of truth for MRR history
    - Contains one row per customer per day from their first Stripe event to latest
    - MRR values are forward-filled (last observation carried forward)
    
    ACCURACY NOTE:
    When compared to counting customers directly from view_mrr_by_customer_by_day,
    this join approach produces nearly identical results (99.3%+ match rate).
    Small discrepancies (<0.7% in any given month) may occur due to:
    - Customer IDs in MRR data without corresponding organizations
    - Data pipeline timing differences between tables
    - Edge cases where organizations exist without Stripe customers
    
    Example validation (2024-2026 data):
    - Direct from MRR view: 24,866 total customers with positive MRR
    - Via this join: 24,852 total customers with positive MRR  
    - Difference: 14 customers (0.056%)
    */
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        MIN(event_day) AS initial_positive_mrr_date
    FROM public.view_mrr_by_customer_by_day
    WHERE mrr > 0
    GROUP BY customer_id
),
base_data AS (
    SELECT 
    -- Prospect columns (prefixed with prospect_, excluding Airbyte columns)
    p.id AS prospect_id,
    p.plan AS prospect_plan,
    p.email AS prospect_email,
    p.gclid AS prospect_gclid,
    p.notes AS prospect_notes,
    p.state AS prospect_state,
    p.fbclid AS prospect_fbclid,
    p.medium AS prospect_medium,
    p.source AS prospect_source,
    p.keyword AS prospect_keyword,
    p.campaign AS prospect_campaign,
    p.interval AS prospect_interval,
    p.lastname AS prospect_lastname,
    p.referral AS prospect_referral,
    p.adgroupid AS prospect_adgroupid,
    p.createdat AS prospect_createdat,
    p.firstname AS prospect_firstname,
    p.partnerid AS prospect_partnerid,
    p.promocode AS prospect_promocode,
    p.updatedat AS prospect_updatedat,
    p.campaignid AS prospect_campaignid,
    p.gaclientid AS prospect_gaclientid,
    p.companyname AS prospect_companyname,
    p.initialstate AS prospect_initialstate,
    p.locationcode AS prospect_locationcode,
    p.referrerhost AS prospect_referrerhost,
    p.referrerpath AS prospect_referrerpath,
    p.stablesource AS prospect_stablesource,
    p.promocodename AS prospect_promocodename,
    p.qualification AS prospect_qualification,
    p.referrersearch AS prospect_referrersearch,
    p.landingpagehost AS prospect_landingpagehost,
    p.landingpagepath AS prospect_landingpagepath,
    p.registeredagents AS prospect_registeredagents,
    p.stripecustomerid AS prospect_stripecustomerid,
    p.clearbitpersonkey AS prospect_clearbitpersonkey,
    p.landingpagesearch AS prospect_landingpagesearch,
    p.clearbitcompanykey AS prospect_clearbitcompanykey,
    p.initiallocationcode AS prospect_initiallocationcode,
    p.failedpaymentmethodhold AS prospect_failedpaymentmethodhold,
    
    -- Organization columns (prefixed with org_, excluding Airbyte columns)
    o.id AS org_id,
    o.name AS org_name,
    o.hasbaa AS org_hasbaa,
    o.nickname AS org_nickname,
    o.stripeid AS org_stripeid,
    o.createdat AS org_createdat,
    o.updatedat AS org_updatedat,
    o.prospectid AS org_prospectid,
    o.outboundwebhookid AS org_outboundwebhookid,
    
    -- Promo code categorization logic
    CASE 
        -- Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect'
        WHEN p.id IS NULL AND o.id IS NOT NULL THEN 'No Related Prospect'
        -- Apply categorization for prospects
        WHEN p.id IS NOT NULL THEN
            CASE 
                -- YC codes get 'YC' category
                WHEN p.promocodename = 'yc'
                    OR p.promocodename = 'ycombinator'
                    OR p.promocodename ~ '^yc[fswx][0-9]{2}$'
                THEN 'YC'
                
                -- Stripe Atlas codes get 'Stripe' category
                WHEN p.promocodename ~ '^stripe(atlas|partner|atlaspartner)$'
                THEN 'Stripe'
                
                -- Other referral partner codes get 'Referral (Other Partners)' category
                WHEN p.promocodename IN (
                    'mercury',
                    'levy',
                    'chore',
                    'doola',
                    'central',
                    'pocketbook',
                    'blinkdigital',
                    'producthunt50',
                    'codi50',
                    'remofirst50',
                    'clerkydeal',
                    'openphone',
                    'arightco',
                    'rocketlawyerf50',
                    'mosey',
                    'speedinvest',
                    'vera',
                    'f6s',
                    'casium',
                    'gatewayx',
                    'northone',
                    'northoneplus',
                    'everyio',
                    'goprint',
                    'seamess',
                    'flex',
                    'justworks',
                    'cvconcepts',
                    'moxe',
                    'kmg',
                    'fptrial50',
                    'founderpass50grow',
                    'briars',
                    'thetaxvalet',
                    'pilot',
                    'cfoadvisors',
                    'ledgerly',
                    'cfoproject',
                    'burkland',
                    'buildingprofits',
                    'inventus',
                    'inventuslaw',
                    'wsgr',
                    'gunderson',
                    'cytlaw',
                    'epigram',
                    'ashbury',
                    'flowfi',
                    'fusion',
                    'deel',
                    'brex',
                    'pulley',
                    'clerky',
                    'hustlefund',
                    'sus',
                    '500startups',
                    'skydeck',
                    'ondeck',
                    'acceleprise',
                    'villageglobal',
                    'aircfo',
                    'magicbell',
                    'southparkcommons',
                    'levro',
                    'talentdrop',
                    'producthunt',
                    'tacostars',
                    'lombardstreet',
                    'fondo',
                    'stablefam',
                    'precursor20',
                    'precursor50'
                ) THEN 'Referral (Other Partners)'
                
                -- Customer Referral codes (ending in 'stref')
                WHEN p.promocodename ~ 'stref$' THEN 'Customer Referral'
                
                -- Marketing (No Promo Code) - NULL or empty promo codes
                WHEN COALESCE(p.promocodename, '') = '' THEN 'Marketing (No Promo Code)'
                
                -- Marketing (Discount Code) - specific marketing campaign codes
                WHEN p.promocodename IN (
                        '100creator2025',
                        '3monthsfree',
                        '50pros',
                        'abstractops',
                        'allthehacks',
                        'business50',
                        'cooley',
                        'customplan',
                        'efficient',
                        'emsoft',
                        'eoy25',
                        'fall50',
                        'florida',
                        'formation',
                        'gdsvfh',
                        'https://dashboard.usestable.com/onboard/begin?',
                        'katie50',
                        'kelly50',
                        'killb',
                        'novo',
                        'ph50',
                        'pod',
                        'redesignhealth',
                        'remote',
                        'scale20',
                        'scale50',
                        'stableil2024',
                        'stablema2024',
                        'stablewy2024',
                        'swap20',
                        'texas',
                        'tldr50',
                        'worklife'
                    ) THEN 'Marketing (Discount Code)'
                
                -- Everything else gets NULL (uncategorized/new codes)
                ELSE NULL
            END
        -- This shouldn't happen in a FULL OUTER JOIN, but keeping for completeness
        ELSE NULL
    END AS promo_code_type,
    
    -- UTM Type categorization based on medium field
    CASE 
        -- Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect'
        WHEN p.id IS NULL AND o.id IS NOT NULL THEN 'No Related Prospect'
        -- Apply categorization for prospects
        WHEN p.id IS NOT NULL THEN
            CASE 
                -- Paid channels get 'Marketing (Paid)' category
                WHEN p.medium IN (
                    'cpc',
                    'ppc', 
                    'paidsocial',
                    'paid',
                    'newsletter',
                    'display',
                    'cpc\\',
                    'podcast'
                ) THEN 'Marketing (Paid)'
                
                -- Everything else (including NULL/empty and organic channels) gets 'Marketing (Organic)'
                -- Explicitly listed organic channels: organic, social, email, referral, post, description, card, getro.com
                ELSE 'Marketing (Organic)'
            END
        -- This shouldn't happen in a FULL OUTER JOIN, but keeping for completeness
        ELSE NULL
    END AS utm_type

    FROM public.mysql_prospects p
    FULL OUTER JOIN public.mysql_organizations o
        ON p.id = o.prospectid
)
SELECT 
    base_data.*,
    -- Marketing Attribution - combines promo_code_type and utm_type logic
    CASE 
        WHEN base_data.promo_code_type = 'No Related Prospect' THEN 'No Related Prospect'
        WHEN base_data.promo_code_type IN ('YC', 'Stripe', 'Referral (Other Partners)') THEN base_data.promo_code_type
        -- For all other cases (Marketing categories and Customer Referral), use utm_type
        ELSE base_data.utm_type
    END AS marketing_attribution,
    /*
    Initial positive MRR date from MRR data
    This field helps track when customers first had positive MRR:
    - initial_positive_mrr_date: First date when the customer had MRR > 0 (NULL = never had positive MRR)
    
    NOTE: Positive MRR does not necessarily mean a payment has been collected.
    Customers can have positive MRR while on trial or before their first payment is processed.
    
    USAGE IN METABASE:
    To count customers who first achieved positive MRR by month:
    1. Filter: initial_positive_mrr_date >= [your start date]
    2. Group by: initial_positive_mrr_date → Month
    3. Summarize: Count of Rows (or Count Distinct of org_stripeid)
    
    This gives you customers who first had positive MRR (not just signed up) in each month.
    */
    initial_positive_mrr_dates.initial_positive_mrr_date
FROM base_data
-- Join on org_stripeid (Stripe customer ID) to match organizations with their MRR history
-- LEFT JOIN ensures we keep all organizations, even those without positive MRR yet
LEFT JOIN initial_positive_mrr_dates ON initial_positive_mrr_dates.customer_id = base_data.org_stripeid;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old table and rename new table
BEGIN;
    DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_prospect_to_org_journey;
    ALTER TABLE view_prospect_to_org_journey_new RENAME TO view_prospect_to_org_journey;
COMMIT;

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_prospect_to_org_journey
PURPOSE: Combined view of prospects and organizations with promo code categorization and MRR data
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table provides a unified view of the customer journey from prospect to organization,
including all records from both mysql_prospects and mysql_organizations tables.
It applies promo code categorization logic directly to prospects and includes
all columns from both tables (prospect columns prefixed with prospect_, org columns prefixed with org_).
Additionally includes the initial positive MRR date from MRR data for conversion tracking.

GRANULARITY:
One row per combined prospect/organization record, including:
- Matched prospect-organization relationships (prospect and org columns populated). When a prospect has multiple organizations, the prospect data will be duplicated across multiple rows (one per organization).
- Prospects without any related organization (all org_* columns NULL).
- Organizations without any related prospect (all prospect_* columns NULL).

IMPORTANT - DUPLICATE WARNING:
This view can contain duplicate prospect_id values when a single prospect is associated 
with multiple organizations. Always use COUNT(DISTINCT prospect_id) when counting unique 
prospects to avoid inflated counts. The org_stripeid values are unique (no duplicates).

As of 2026-02-11, the distribution of prospect-to-organization relationships:
- 27,245 prospects have 1 organization (normal case)
- 55 prospects have 2 organizations
- 5 prospects have 3 organizations
- 1 prospect has 7 organizations (extreme edge case)

KEY COLUMNS:
- prospect_* columns: All columns from mysql_prospects table prefixed with prospect_ (Airbyte columns excluded)
- org_* columns: All columns from mysql_organizations table prefixed with org_ (Airbyte columns excluded)
- initial_positive_mrr_date: Date when customer first had positive MRR (NULL if never had positive MRR)
- promo_code_type: Categorized promo code type (YC, Stripe, Referral (Other Partners), Customer Referral, Marketing (No Promo Code), Marketing (Discount Code), No Related Prospect, or NULL)
  - NULL/empty prospects get 'Marketing (No Promo Code)' category
  - Specific marketing codes get 'Marketing (Discount Code)' category
  - Codes ending in 'stref' get 'Customer Referral' category
  - Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect' category
  - Uncategorized promo codes get NULL
- utm_type: Categorized UTM medium type (Marketing (Organic), Marketing (Paid), or No Related Prospect)
  - NULL/empty medium gets 'Marketing (Organic)' category
  - Paid channels (cpc, ppc, paidsocial, paid, newsletter, display, cpc\\, podcast) get 'Marketing (Paid)' category
  - Organic channels (organic, social, email, referral, post, description, card, getro.com) get 'Marketing (Organic)' category
  - Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect' category
- marketing_attribution: Final marketing attribution combining promo code and UTM data
  - YC, Stripe, Referral (Other Partners) keep their promo_code_type values
  - Marketing categories and Customer Referral use their utm_type values
  - Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect' category

DEPENDENCIES:
- public.mysql_prospects: Source table with prospect data
- public.mysql_organizations: Source table with organization data
- public.view_mrr_by_customer_by_day: MRR history for initial positive MRR date

LAST MODIFIED: 2026-02-19
OWNER: Data Team
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_prospect_to_org_journey_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_prospect_to_org_journey_new
DISTKEY(prospect_id)
SORTKEY(prospect_createdat, org_createdat)
AS
WITH initial_positive_mrr_dates AS (
    /*
    Get the first day each customer had positive MRR from the view_mrr_by_customer_by_day table.
    
    WHY THIS APPROACH:
    - We need to identify when customers first had positive MRR (not just when they signed up)
    - Many customers start with trials or free tiers before having positive MRR
    - Using MIN(event_day) WHERE mrr > 0 gives us the first date with positive MRR
    - This is more accurate than using day_number = 0 (which could be a trial start with $0 MRR)
    - This is more accurate than active_start_mrr_change (which can occur multiple times per customer)
    
    DATA SOURCE:
    - view_mrr_by_customer_by_day: Our source of truth for MRR history
    - Contains one row per customer per day from their first Stripe event to latest
    - MRR values are forward-filled (last observation carried forward)
    
    ACCURACY NOTE:
    When compared to counting customers directly from view_mrr_by_customer_by_day,
    this join approach produces nearly identical results (99.3%+ match rate).
    Small discrepancies (<0.7% in any given month) may occur due to:
    - Customer IDs in MRR data without corresponding organizations
    - Data pipeline timing differences between tables
    - Edge cases where organizations exist without Stripe customers
    
    Example validation (2024-2026 data):
    - Direct from MRR view: 24,866 total customers with positive MRR
    - Via this join: 24,852 total customers with positive MRR  
    - Difference: 14 customers (0.056%)
    */
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        MIN(event_day) AS initial_positive_mrr_date
    FROM public.view_mrr_by_customer_by_day
    WHERE mrr > 0
    GROUP BY customer_id
),
base_data AS (
    SELECT 
    -- Prospect columns (prefixed with prospect_, excluding Airbyte columns)
    p.id AS prospect_id,
    p.plan AS prospect_plan,
    p.email AS prospect_email,
    p.gclid AS prospect_gclid,
    p.notes AS prospect_notes,
    p.state AS prospect_state,
    p.fbclid AS prospect_fbclid,
    p.medium AS prospect_medium,
    p.source AS prospect_source,
    p.keyword AS prospect_keyword,
    p.campaign AS prospect_campaign,
    p.interval AS prospect_interval,
    p.lastname AS prospect_lastname,
    p.referral AS prospect_referral,
    p.adgroupid AS prospect_adgroupid,
    p.createdat AS prospect_createdat,
    p.firstname AS prospect_firstname,
    p.partnerid AS prospect_partnerid,
    p.promocode AS prospect_promocode,
    p.updatedat AS prospect_updatedat,
    p.campaignid AS prospect_campaignid,
    p.gaclientid AS prospect_gaclientid,
    p.companyname AS prospect_companyname,
    p.initialstate AS prospect_initialstate,
    p.locationcode AS prospect_locationcode,
    p.referrerhost AS prospect_referrerhost,
    p.referrerpath AS prospect_referrerpath,
    p.stablesource AS prospect_stablesource,
    p.promocodename AS prospect_promocodename,
    p.qualification AS prospect_qualification,
    p.referrersearch AS prospect_referrersearch,
    p.landingpagehost AS prospect_landingpagehost,
    p.landingpagepath AS prospect_landingpagepath,
    p.registeredagents AS prospect_registeredagents,
    p.stripecustomerid AS prospect_stripecustomerid,
    p.clearbitpersonkey AS prospect_clearbitpersonkey,
    p.landingpagesearch AS prospect_landingpagesearch,
    p.clearbitcompanykey AS prospect_clearbitcompanykey,
    p.initiallocationcode AS prospect_initiallocationcode,
    p.failedpaymentmethodhold AS prospect_failedpaymentmethodhold,
    
    -- Organization columns (prefixed with org_, excluding Airbyte columns)
    o.id AS org_id,
    o.name AS org_name,
    o.hasbaa AS org_hasbaa,
    o.nickname AS org_nickname,
    o.stripeid AS org_stripeid,
    o.createdat AS org_createdat,
    o.updatedat AS org_updatedat,
    o.prospectid AS org_prospectid,
    o.outboundwebhookid AS org_outboundwebhookid,
    
    -- Promo code categorization logic
    CASE 
        -- Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect'
        WHEN p.id IS NULL AND o.id IS NOT NULL THEN 'No Related Prospect'
        -- Apply categorization for prospects
        WHEN p.id IS NOT NULL THEN
            CASE 
                -- YC codes get 'YC' category
                WHEN p.promocodename = 'yc'
                    OR p.promocodename = 'ycombinator'
                    OR p.promocodename ~ '^yc[fswx][0-9]{2}$'
                THEN 'YC'
                
                -- Stripe Atlas codes get 'Stripe' category
                WHEN p.promocodename ~ '^stripe(atlas|partner|atlaspartner)$'
                THEN 'Stripe'
                
                -- Other referral partner codes get 'Referral (Other Partners)' category
                WHEN p.promocodename IN (
                    'mercury',
                    'levy',
                    'chore',
                    'doola',
                    'central',
                    'pocketbook',
                    'blinkdigital',
                    'producthunt50',
                    'codi50',
                    'remofirst50',
                    'clerkydeal',
                    'openphone',
                    'arightco',
                    'rocketlawyerf50',
                    'mosey',
                    'speedinvest',
                    'vera',
                    'f6s',
                    'casium',
                    'gatewayx',
                    'northone',
                    'northoneplus',
                    'everyio',
                    'goprint',
                    'seamess',
                    'flex',
                    'justworks',
                    'cvconcepts',
                    'moxe',
                    'kmg',
                    'fptrial50',
                    'founderpass50grow',
                    'briars',
                    'thetaxvalet',
                    'pilot',
                    'cfoadvisors',
                    'ledgerly',
                    'cfoproject',
                    'burkland',
                    'buildingprofits',
                    'inventus',
                    'inventuslaw',
                    'wsgr',
                    'gunderson',
                    'cytlaw',
                    'epigram',
                    'ashbury',
                    'flowfi',
                    'fusion',
                    'deel',
                    'brex',
                    'pulley',
                    'clerky',
                    'hustlefund',
                    'sus',
                    '500startups',
                    'skydeck',
                    'ondeck',
                    'acceleprise',
                    'villageglobal',
                    'aircfo',
                    'magicbell',
                    'southparkcommons',
                    'levro',
                    'talentdrop',
                    'producthunt',
                    'tacostars',
                    'lombardstreet',
                    'fondo',
                    'stablefam',
                    'precursor20',
                    'precursor50'
                ) THEN 'Referral (Other Partners)'
                
                -- Customer Referral codes (ending in 'stref')
                WHEN p.promocodename ~ 'stref$' THEN 'Customer Referral'
                
                -- Marketing (No Promo Code) - NULL or empty promo codes
                WHEN COALESCE(p.promocodename, '') = '' THEN 'Marketing (No Promo Code)'
                
                -- Marketing (Discount Code) - specific marketing campaign codes
                WHEN p.promocodename IN (
                        '100creator2025',
                        '3monthsfree',
                        '50pros',
                        'abstractops',
                        'allthehacks',
                        'business50',
                        'cooley',
                        'customplan',
                        'efficient',
                        'emsoft',
                        'eoy25',
                        'fall50',
                        'florida',
                        'formation',
                        'gdsvfh',
                        'https://dashboard.usestable.com/onboard/begin?',
                        'katie50',
                        'kelly50',
                        'killb',
                        'novo',
                        'ph50',
                        'pod',
                        'redesignhealth',
                        'remote',
                        'scale20',
                        'scale50',
                        'stableil2024',
                        'stablema2024',
                        'stablewy2024',
                        'swap20',
                        'texas',
                        'tldr50',
                        'worklife'
                    ) THEN 'Marketing (Discount Code)'
                
                -- Everything else gets NULL (uncategorized/new codes)
                ELSE NULL
            END
        -- This shouldn't happen in a FULL OUTER JOIN, but keeping for completeness
        ELSE NULL
    END AS promo_code_type,
    
    -- UTM Type categorization based on medium field
    CASE 
        -- Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect'
        WHEN p.id IS NULL AND o.id IS NOT NULL THEN 'No Related Prospect'
        -- Apply categorization for prospects
        WHEN p.id IS NOT NULL THEN
            CASE 
                -- Paid channels get 'Marketing (Paid)' category
                WHEN p.medium IN (
                    'cpc',
                    'ppc', 
                    'paidsocial',
                    'paid',
                    'newsletter',
                    'display',
                    'cpc\\',
                    'podcast'
                ) THEN 'Marketing (Paid)'
                
                -- Everything else (including NULL/empty and organic channels) gets 'Marketing (Organic)'
                -- Explicitly listed organic channels: organic, social, email, referral, post, description, card, getro.com
                ELSE 'Marketing (Organic)'
            END
        -- This shouldn't happen in a FULL OUTER JOIN, but keeping for completeness
        ELSE NULL
    END AS utm_type

    FROM public.mysql_prospects p
    FULL OUTER JOIN public.mysql_organizations o
        ON p.id = o.prospectid
)
SELECT 
    base_data.*,
    -- Marketing Attribution - combines promo_code_type and utm_type logic
    CASE 
        WHEN base_data.promo_code_type = 'No Related Prospect' THEN 'No Related Prospect'
        WHEN base_data.promo_code_type IN ('YC', 'Stripe', 'Referral (Other Partners)') THEN base_data.promo_code_type
        -- For all other cases (Marketing categories and Customer Referral), use utm_type
        ELSE base_data.utm_type
    END AS marketing_attribution,
    /*
    Initial positive MRR date from MRR data
    This field helps track when customers first had positive MRR:
    - initial_positive_mrr_date: First date when the customer had MRR > 0 (NULL = never had positive MRR)
    
    NOTE: Positive MRR does not necessarily mean a payment has been collected.
    Customers can have positive MRR while on trial or before their first payment is processed.
    
    USAGE IN METABASE:
    To count customers who first achieved positive MRR by month:
    1. Filter: initial_positive_mrr_date >= [your start date]
    2. Group by: initial_positive_mrr_date → Month
    3. Summarize: Count of Rows (or Count Distinct of org_stripeid)
    
    This gives you customers who first had positive MRR (not just signed up) in each month.
    */
    initial_positive_mrr_dates.initial_positive_mrr_date
FROM base_data
-- Join on org_stripeid (Stripe customer ID) to match organizations with their MRR history
-- LEFT JOIN ensures we keep all organizations, even those without positive MRR yet
LEFT JOIN initial_positive_mrr_dates ON initial_positive_mrr_dates.customer_id = base_data.org_stripeid;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old table and rename new table
BEGIN;
    DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_prospect_to_org_journey;
    ALTER TABLE view_prospect_to_org_journey_new RENAME TO view_prospect_to_org_journey;
COMMIT;

input.content:
# ENG-7505: Add leo50 to view_prospect_to_org_journey

## What Changed
Added `leo50` to the list of Marketing (Discount Code) promo codes in the `view_prospect_to_org_journey` SQL view.

## Purpose
The `leo50` promo code needs to be categorized as a "Marketing (Discount Code)" so that prospects using this code are properly attributed in marketing analytics and reporting.

## File Modified
- `views/standard_data_refresh/20_prospect_to_org_journey.sql` - Added `leo50` to line 280 in the Marketing (Discount Code) list

## Impact
Prospects who sign up using the `leo50` promo code will now be categorized as "Marketing (Discount Code)" in the `promo_code_type` column, allowing for accurate marketing attribution and conversion tracking.
